data.title: Bitcoin Treasury Corporation Completes Initial Phase of Bitcoin Acquisition
data.description:
According to PANews, Bitcoin Treasury Corporation, a company listed in Canada, has announced the completion of the initial phase of its Bitcoin acquisition plan. The company has purchased 478.57 Bitcoins at a total cost of 70 million Canadian dollars, approximately 51.16 million U.S. dollars. Currently, the company holds 771.37 Bitcoins on its balance sheet. This acquisition is part of a broader strategy to enhance long-term shareholder value.

Previously, Bitcoin Treasury Corporation, listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange, completed its first purchase of 292.8 Bitcoins.

data.title: U.S. Bitcoin Spot ETFs Experience Significant Inflows
data.description: According to BlockBeats, U.S. Bitcoin spot ETFs have seen a net inflow of $2.214 billion this week. The breakdown of inflows includes BlackRock's IBIT with $1.31 billion, Fidelity's FBTC with $504.5 million, Bitwise's BITB with $74.1 million, ARK's ARKB with $268 million, Invesco's BTCO with $9.9 million, Franklin's EZBC with $6.2 million, and VanEck's HODL with $21.1 million. Meanwhile, Grayscale's GBTC experienced a net outflow of $5.7 million, while Grayscale Mini BTC saw an inflow of $25.6 million.

data.title: Bitcoin Spot ETFs See Significant Inflows on June 27
data.description:
According to Foresight News, data from SoSoValue indicates that Bitcoin spot ETFs experienced a total net inflow of $501 million on June 27, Eastern Time.

The Fidelity ETF FBTC led the inflows with a single-day net inflow of $166 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $11.961 billion. Following closely, the Blackrock ETF IBIT recorded a net inflow of $153 million, with its historical total net inflow reaching $52.309 billion.

As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs stands at $133.17 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio of 6.25% relative to Bitcoin's total market capitalization. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $48.872 billion.

data.title: Circle's USDC Circulation Increases by 400 Million in Recent Week
data.description: According to PANews, official data reveals that Circle issued approximately 3.4 billion USDC and redeemed around 3.1 billion USDC in the seven days leading up to June 26, resulting in a net increase of about 400 million USDC in circulation. The total circulation of USDC now stands at 61.6 billion, with reserves amounting to approximately 61.9 billion USD. This reserve includes about 8.6 billion USD in cash and 53.3 billion USD held in the Circle Reserve Fund.

data.title: Endless Clouds Foundation announces END token economics: total supply 500 million, 17.5% airdropped
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Endless Clouds Foundation published an article on the X platform to announce the END token economics: the total supply is 500 million, the initial circulation supply is 155,500,000 (accounting for 31.1%), and most of the token supply is dedicated to community rewards. In addition, the team token allocation accounts for 20%, the ecological fund allocation accounts for 10%, the airdrop accounts for 17.5%, and the community accounts for 32.45%.

data.title: Cardano On the Edge: Will $0.47 Be the Bounce or the Break?
data.description:
"Cardano is currently navigating a critical juncture in its price movement, prompting speculation and analysis around the significant threshold of $0.47. Investors and traders alike are pondering whether this level will serve as a supportive bounce point, leading to a potential resurgence in the cryptocurrency's value, or if it will ultimately give way to a breakdown, resulting in further declines in its market price."
source: https://blocknews.com/cardano-on-the-edge-will-0-47-be-the-bounce-or-the-break/

data.title: Chairman of the U.S. Digital Asset Financial Advisory Committee: The proportion of cryptocurrency portfolios can increase to 10-40%
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Ric Edelman, chairman of the Digital Assets Council of Financial Advisors, said in an interview with CNBC that it is recommended to allocate 10% to 40% of investment funds to cryptocurrencies, while four years ago his recommendation was 1%. Ric Edelman said that four years ago, he did not know whether the US government would ban Bitcoin, or whether the technology would become obsolete, and whether consumers and institutions would accept and adopt Bitcoin, but now all these problems have been solved, and the price of Bitcoin does not move in sync with the prices of stocks, bonds, gold, oil or commodities. The crypto asset class offers higher return opportunities than almost any other asset class. (CNBC)

data.title: Arthur Hayes deposited 2.468 million ETHFI into Binance in the past 5 hours, making a profit of $1.02 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst Ember, BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes deposited 2.468 million ETHFI (worth approximately $2.21 million) into Binance in the past 5 hours, making a profit of $1.02 million.
This time, Arthur Hayes bought 2.376 million ETHFI at a low point in April through FlowDesk at a price of $0.5.
